stories.headline: ‘The Ring’ star Daveigh Chase left behind substantial estate worth 6 figures - Page Six
stories.summary: The former child star was living on the streets of Downtown Los Angeles at the time of her death.
stories.why: A cautionary tale about child stardom's dark aftermath—the guy who watched her in 'The Ring' as a kid is now confronting what happened to her, raising uncomfortable questions about how Hollywood discards young talent.

stories.headline: Justin Baldoni Opposes Blake Lively's $8 Million Legal Fee Request - TMZ
stories.summary: Justin Baldoni is firing back at Blake Lively's request that he pay a seven-figure sum to cover her legal bills in their "It Ends With Us" battle ... TMZ has learned.
stories.why: This signals a major power shift in Hollywood harassment cases: Blake Lively's willingness to go nuclear on a co-star despite studio pressure shows A-listers are no longer protecting abusers, which changes the calculus for how men in power operate on set.

stories.headline: Paramount's David Ellison Meets With House Members On Tax Incentive - Deadline
stories.summary: Ellison's meeting was taking place on the same day that Democratic attorneys general sued to block the Paramount-WB merger.
stories.why: Ellison's lobbying while facing antitrust action reveals how mega-mergers happen anyway—a reminder that corporate consolidation in entertainment keeps accelerating regardless of political theater, directly affecting what movies and shows he'll have access to.

stories.headline: California, 11 other states sue to block Paramount's takeover of Warner Bros - KCRA
stories.summary: Twelve states sued to block Paramount's takeover of Warner Bros. Discovery on Monday, warning that the $81 billion mega merger would "extinguish competition" in Hollywood and threaten jobs across the…
stories.why: This isn't just regulatory noise: fewer studios mean fewer career paths for directors, writers, and crew, plus consolidated creative control that makes the industry less competitive—directly impacting whether original ideas or just sequels get greenlit.

stories.headline: Global Box Office: 'Moana' No Waves at $95M WW, 'Kung Fu Soccer' Surprises - Deadline
stories.summary: 'Moana' like in the U.S. failed to catch a wave at the global box office, while Stephen Chow's 'Kung Fu Soccer' kicks its way into second overall.
stories.why: Disney's marquee franchise underperforming globally signals that IP fatigue is real and studios can't just pump out sequels anymore; this matters for what gets funded next and whether risky, original projects survive the next round of cuts.

stories.headline: ‘How to Eat Fried Worms’ child star Blake Garrett’s cause of death revealed - Page Six
stories.summary: The actor died in February at the age of 33.
stories.why: Another child actor dead at 33 from a seemingly normal life—this compounds the pattern that's forcing a reckoning about child labor protections in Hollywood, something that affects how future projects involving minors get made.

stories.headline: 'Hero, legend, sweetheart': Tributes to Jurassic Park actor Sam Neill, who has died aged 78 - BBC
stories.summary: Friends, politicians and fellow film stars remember Neill following his "sudden and unexpected" death.
stories.why: Sam Neill's death marks the end of an era for practical-effects action cinema and the last of a generation of charismatic leading men, signaling Hollywood's full pivot to CGI spectacle and younger, more algorithm-friendly stars.

stories.headline: ‘Avengers: Doomsday’ Tickets to Go On Sale Next Week, Runtime Revealed (Exclusive) - The Hollywood Reporter
stories.summary: 'Avengers: Doomsday' tickets are going on sale early for premium format screens — and it has an epic runtime (though it's not final).
stories.why: A 3+ hour superhero film still commands early premium ticket sales despite audience fatigue, proving Marvel's brand loyalty still hits, but the bloated runtime signals studios are prioritizing spectacle over story—the template for tent poles he'll see for years.
